signals.reasoning: Keep managing the live ARB long. It is still sitting at peak P&L, has no giveback pressure at all, and the board remains strongly bullish enough to support the position despite the veto state.
BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has a mild bullish lean, but not enough to justify fresh risk under the veto regime.
SOL has weakened enough that it no longer belongs in the buy bucket, but it is not cleanly bearish either.
FOGO remains the clearest bearish name on the board because both price and mechanics continue to lean decisively negative.
DASH remains one of the best long candidates on pure mechanics, but veto should keep it in watch mode rather than as a fresh buy.
USTC remains too thin and noisy to justify action.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, so even though the board is bullish it is not clean enough to trade under veto.
TNSR is a modest bearish lean because both price and mechanics have continued to roll over and there is no real recovery structure yet.
